title: unspoken words
wikitext:
[]: unspoken words             Quote Post by NERV » Sun Jul 24, 2005 2:55 pm One question i bet most of you know the answer to. its been on my mind for awhile now but anyways. In EOE when Gendo Ikari has the gun pointed at Ritsuko, When the magi doesnt destroy itself Ikari says something like "the truth is..." then you see his lips move but theres no words then he shoots ritsuko. what the hell does he say???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 24, 2005 3:04 pm This is still an open issue. Two strong contenders are 1) "The truth is, you're just like your old woman." She has become just like her mother, Naoko, who was brushed aside as an old hag. 2) "The truth is, I didn't know what they would do." Gendo is genuinely sorry for her rape by SEELE's minions.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Sun Jul 24, 2005 3:24 pm I believe that Shin-seiki had some kind of theory on that... "The truth is, you were never Rei's replacement." I may be wrong, though.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jul 24, 2005 5:17 pm My line on this is we should pay more attention to what he did say, rather than what he didn't. He uses the -kun honorific here for Ritsuko. This is one of only four occassions where Gendou uses an honorific, three of which involved Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by NERV » Sun Jul 24, 2005 5:17 pm THnx a lot

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Sun Jul 24, 2005 6:13 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: My line on this is we should pay more attention to what he did say, rather than what he didn't. He uses the -kun honorific here for Ritsuko. This is one of only four occassions where Gendou uses an honorific, three of which involved Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 24, 2005 9:02 pm Instead of laying out your whole "-kun" argument again, OMF, you could just provide a link...

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jul 24, 2005 10:10 pm I've got an idea on this, and this is a good place to try it out I guss. Now we know that Ristusko's face is animated the same way during the Seele interogation, as when gendo utters his " unheard line" . Now during the Seele scene, ritsuko's got " that look" on her face when they tell her that gendo sent her to them in Rei's place, and this seems to effect her more then being naked In EoE she's got the same look on her face as Gendo said " The truth is . . . " ,, now cutting the end of old Gendo's line thier seems to put a lot of weight on the part we do hear. " the truth is " . Now look back on the Seele scene, and think about somthing. Gendo DId sende her their in Rei's place, and it seem to be somthing that effects her a great deal. More infact then being naked in a room with Seele . It seem the truth in reguards to Gendo hurts the good doctor a bit yes? To rap this rant up what if the two scenes are their to show that Ritsuko was lieing to herself about Gendo the entire time they were together ( years). She was willing to go through anything for him and yet he picks others over her. naimly Rei, as a way to get Yui back. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Mon Jul 25, 2005 10:54 am Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 25, 2005 10:58 am Seele said that gendo senrt her in Rei's place.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jul 25, 2005 11:09 am Reichu wrote: Instead of laying out your whole "-kun" argument again, OMF, you could just provide a link...

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Mon Jul 25, 2005 4:47 pm I thought that gendo was suppoed to say something like dr. ritsuko kaga(blah blah) I (silenced). then shoots her. And from where I read this (forgot... maybe a previus thread) this last I was supposed to be a hint form anno in a interview or something of the sort because he didn't wana reveal the whole truth. anyways I'm not sure if its true or not I'm only speaking halfassed so if i'm wrong dont bother flaming eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Mon Jul 25, 2005 7:16 pm TheBlueTree wrote: I thought that gendo was suppoed to say something like dr. ritsuko kaga(blah blah) I (silenced). then shoots her. And from where I read this (forgot... maybe a previus thread) this last I was supposed to be a hint form anno in a interview or something of the sort because he didn't wana reveal the whole truth. anyways I'm not sure if its true or not I'm only speaking halfassed so if i'm wrong dont bother flaming eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Wed Jul 27, 2005 4:23 pm Mr. Tines wrote: This is still an open issue. Two strong contenders are 2) "The truth is, I didn't know what they would do." Gendo is genuinely sorry for her rape by SEELE's minions.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 27, 2005 4:40 pm Shnooks wrote: Ritsuko got raped? I knew she said something about letting him have his way, but I thought it was with Gendo. And the scene where she was nakkie in front of the Seele members, i didn't know they raped her.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Jul 29, 2005 11:16 pm OK SO WHAT!!!! first i find that Asuka gets raped in a mind thingy and now risuko is raped WHAT?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 30, 2005 1:45 am For Asuka, it's purely a psychic violation - not that that is any better; and then the next episode, Rei/EVA-00 get tentacle raped on screen. Ritsuko's implied sufferings, being much more traditional, happen off-screen. Nobody escapes the series undamaged.

[]:             Quote Post by sir_evangelist » Sat Jul 30, 2005 10:29 am hi, IÂ´m new and this is my first answer: First of all, in episode 23, ritsuko says: Yeah, I know. Destruction. These are not humans, but are humanshaped things. I lost against THE THING. I could not win. When he is in my mind, I can bear any insult. I don't care about my own body. But he, he did, ... I knew that. I am stupid, I am as stupid as my mother. Very stupid. You can kill me, if you wish. I am glad if you do that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 30, 2005 12:57 pm It should also be noted that in Japanese society, rape is one of the ultimate forms of humiliation. Most rape victims do nothing, they keep quiet to hide their shame, and rarely do they seek justice, even if they know the perpetrator. Many also commit suicide as a result.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 30, 2005 5:23 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It should also be noted that in Japanese society, rape is one of the ultimate forms of humiliation. Most rape victims do nothing, they keep quiet to hide their shame, and rarely do they seek justice, even if they know the perpetrator. Many also commit suicide as a result.
